title: MEDIUM: read endpoints never enforce read scope
description: Every endpoint that uses AuthenticatedAccount but performs read-only operations (verify_credentials, get_lists, list_filters, list_sessions, get_push_subscription, etc.) never calls require_scope('read'). A token with scope 'write' (only) can read all user data. The Mastodon API spec expects read-only tokens to exist and be enforced. All authenticated read endpoints should call auth.require_scope('read'). See all occurrences of 'auth: AuthenticatedAccount' without a corresponding require_scope call in src/api.rs, src/push.rs, src/interactions.rs, src/posting.rs.
close_reason: Wontfix: Mastodon API convention. Most clients request read+write. Enforcing read scope would break existing clients.
